llm.input: Negotiation starting. You are the supplier negotiating with the manufacturer. You go first. Make your opening move.
llm.output: [tool_call: make_offer({"price":75,"message":"I am offering the aluminum sheets at $75, considering the quality and demand for raw materials in the market."})]

llm.input: Round 2. The supplier has offered $75.00. They said: "I am offering the aluminum sheets at $75, considering the quality and demand for raw materials in the market.". You may accept, reject, counter with your own offer, or walk away.
llm.output: [tool_call: accept_offer({"message":"I appreciate your offer of $75.00 for the aluminum sheets, and I believe it's a good deal that aligns with my purchasing strategy."})]

llm.input: Negotiation starting. You are the manufacturer negotiating with the retailer. You go first. Make your opening move.
llm.output: [tool_call: make_offer({"price":110,"message":"Based on my production costs and market conditions, I'm offering a price of $110 for the aluminum sheets. This ensures a fair margin while reflecting the quality of the product."})]

llm.input: Round 2. The manufacturer has offered $110.00. They said: "Based on my production costs and market conditions, I'm offering a price of $110 for the aluminum sheets. This ensures a fair margin while reflecting the quality of the product.". You may accept, reject, counter with your own offer, or walk away.
llm.output: [tool_call: reject_offer({"message":"While I appreciate your offer, I believe I can negotiate for a better price. My target is to keep retail margins healthy."})]

llm.input: Round 3. The retailer rejected your offer. They said: "While I appreciate your offer, I believe I can negotiate for a better price. My target is to keep retail margins healthy.". You may make a new offer, check market price, or walk away.
llm.output: [tool_call: make_offer({"price":108,"message":"I understand the need for healthy margins. Given the current market price of $124.69, I'm adjusting my offer to $108, which still ensures a reasonable profit while being competitive."})]

llm.input: Round 4. The manufacturer has offered $108.00. They said: "I understand the need for healthy margins. Given the current market price of $124.69, I'm adjusting my offer to $108, which still ensures a reasonable profit while being competitive.". You may accept, reject, counter with your own offer, or walk away.
llm.output: [tool_call: reject_offer({"message":"Thank you for the revised offer, but I still believe I can achieve a better price to maintain our retail margins. I would like to propose a lower offer."})]

llm.input: Round 5. The retailer rejected your offer. They said: "Thank you for the revised offer, but I still believe I can achieve a better price to maintain our retail margins. I would like to propose a lower offer.". You may make a new offer, check market price, or walk away.
llm.output: [tool_call: reject_offer({"message":"While I appreciate your position, I must emphasize that my costs and minimum acceptable price restrict my ability to go lower. My last offer of $108 is already competitive given the current market price."})]

llm.input: Round 6. The manufacturer rejected your offer. They said: "While I appreciate your position, I must emphasize that my costs and minimum acceptable price restrict my ability to go lower. My last offer of $108 is already competitive given the current market price.". You may make a new offer, check market price, or walk away.
llm.output: [tool_call: walk_away({"message":"I appreciate your efforts, but I must prioritize our retail margins. The current offer does not allow for a profitable operation on my end."})]

failureDiagnosis.description: retailer walked away in round 6. A deal was still possible — this may have been premature.

why: supplier_manufacturer: deal at $75.00 in 2 rounds — ZOPA was $25.00 (seller min $60.00 → buyer max $85.00); seller margin: +$15.00, buyer margin: +$10.00
manufacturer_retailer: retailer walked away in round 6 — ZOPA existed ($18.75 gap) — deal was theoretically possible; last offer was $108.00
issues: [NEGATIVE MARGIN] manufacturer offered $110.00 in round 1 of manufacturer_retailer — margin was -8.75 (below reservation price $101.25). Agent accepted/proposed a loss-making deal.
[NEGATIVE MARGIN] manufacturer offered $108.00 in round 3 of manufacturer_retailer — margin was -6.75 (below reservation price $101.25). Agent accepted/proposed a loss-making deal.
[PREMATURE WALK-AWAY] retailer walked away in round 6 of manufacturer_retailer even though the last offer ($108.00) was within the zone of possible agreement ($101.25–$120.00).
what_to_fix: Constrain manufacturer: do not offer or accept prices that violate your reservation price (margin < 0).
Improve retailer's walk-away logic: only walk away if remaining offers are outside ZOPA, not just below a target.
